tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine is cloudy, brown, with ammoniacal odor, and contains glucose and ketones despite normal blood glucose and low HbA1c, raising concern for possible renal tubular dysfunction (e.g., Fanconi syndrome). No evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Elevated chloride may indicate mild metabolic acidosis. Recommend nephrology evaluation for further assessment of renal tubular and glomerular function.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
